{{Quote|They use those mostly for the ones they send into battle. The thing is, if they fit you with the stuff, it takes a lot out of you. Makes you dumb. The shapers don't want dumb slaves that keep forgetting directions. The warriors just need bodies to absorb blasterfire, so it doesn't really matter there. But act up, or act stupid, and they'll fit you with it and send you to the front.|Poy to Anakin Solo|<ref name="Conquest" />}}
'''Poy''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] of the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] alongside the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Anakin Solo]]<ref name="Conquest" /> in [[26 ABY/Legends|26 ABY]].<ref name="TERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> During their time together as slaves, Solo asked Poy about the differences between the [[Yorik-Kul|implants]] he had seen used on Yuuzhan Vong slaves on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]] and the implants used on Yavin 4. Poy explained that the implants Solo saw on Dantooine were used on those the [[warrior caste]] planned to send into battle, because they destroyed the ability of the slaves to think. Because Poy and the others enslaved on Yavin 4 served the [[shaper caste]], who needed their slaves intelligent enough to follow directions, their implants only conditioned them to obey and caused them pain. He warned Solo that if he acted out, the shapers would fit him with the implants used by the warriors' slaves and send him to the [[Yuuzhan Vong War|war]] front to fight.<ref name="Conquest" />

==Behind the scenes==
Poy first appeared in the [[2001]] novel ''[[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory I: Conquest]]'', written by [[Greg Keyes]].<ref name="Conquest" />
